## reference · layout regions hierarchy whitespace

* A layout is composed of regions, which are areas of the screen that contain content.
* Regions can be grouped into a hierarchy, with some regions nested within others.
* Whitespace (also known as padding or margin) is used to create visual separation between regions and to define the relationships between them.
* A general guideline for whitespace is to use a consistent spacing method throughout the layout.

Sources: https://m2.material.io/design/layout/understanding-layout.html
